Bead agent-4b4cc572 (FZ-063 Tag Fizeau pre-release) — no code changes needed.

Status: AC already satisfied by existing tag and published GitHub release.

AC: "A pre-release tag exists for the renamed module and release artifacts
are available per FZ-032/FZ-033/FZ-034."

Evidence:

(a) Pre-release tag exists for the renamed module
    - `git tag -l "*fizeau*"` → `v0.9.26-fizeau.1`
    - `git show v0.9.26-fizeau.1` → annotated tag "Fizeau pre-release
      v0.9.26-fizeau.1", tagger Erik LaBianca, dated 2026-04-30, pointing at
      commit f241e258 on the renamed module path
      `github.com/easel/fizeau` (per `go.mod`).
    - Downstream confirmation: FZ-061 (agent-f533bff7, closed) records that
      the DDx repo successfully consumed `github.com/easel/fizeau
      v0.9.26-fizeau.1`, proving the tag is resolvable as a Go module
      pre-release.

(b) Release artifacts are available per FZ-032/FZ-033/FZ-034
    - `gh release view v0.9.26-fizeau.1` shows the GitHub release published
      as `prerelease: true` with all four expected fiz-renamed assets:
        * fiz-darwin-amd64
        * fiz-darwin-arm64
        * fiz-linux-amd64
        * fiz-linux-arm64
    - These match the artifact-name contract from FZ-032 (release workflow
      renamed ddx-agent-* → fiz-*, closed via commit 3c381250) and are
      consumed by the renamed installer (FZ-033, closed via a674a340) and
      updater (FZ-034, closed via ae3f23f3).

Conclusion: the pre-release tag has already been pushed and the GitHub
release is live with the FZ-032/033/034-compliant artifacts. There is
nothing additional to commit in this worktree — re-tagging or re-publishing
would be destructive and unauthorized. The bead can be closed on the
strength of the existing tag and release.
